key_points: The movement of celestial bodies helps humans measure time
The rotation of the Earth causes day and night
One complete rotation of the Earth takes about 24 hours
The revolution of the Earth around the Sun determines the length of a year
The phases of the Moon helped early people measure months
Constellations are groups of stars that form patterns in the night sky
examples: Ancient people used sundials to measure time during the day
The Moon completes one cycle of phases in about 29.5 days
Ursa Major is a well-known constellation
The North Star helps in finding the north direction
formulas: 1 day = Time taken by Earth to complete one rotation
1 year = Time taken by Earth to complete one revolution around the Sun
exam_tips: Remember the difference between rotation and revolution of the Earth
Learn examples of constellations such as Ursa Major and Orion
Understand how the phases of the Moon helped in measuring months
Know the role of the Sun and Moon in early time measurement
